steps.title: Use the deep-water clue
steps.content: Reyes-Mendoza and colleagues found that layers more than 400 meters deep had density and salinity conditions like those of the nearby Caribbean Sea. The suggestion should explain that similarity.

steps.title: Reject unsupported leaps
steps.content: Choice B compares variation across layers without support. Choice C jumps to reevaluating other blue holes. Choice D says the relationship is inverse, contradicting the stated similarity to Caribbean conditions.

steps.title: Choose underground connection
steps.content: Tunnels or caves connecting the blue hole to Caribbean waters would explain why very deep layers show Caribbean-like density and salinity. The answer is A.
